Section Header: The following 19 questions screen each body system. A 'Yes' answer opens a detailed section.
Field Label: Have you been told that there were issues during your biological mother's PREGNANCY with you and/or your DELIVERY/BIRTH?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Yes | 2, No | 3, Unsure
Field Note: Examples: mother on bed rest, concerns on ultrasound, born early (prematurely), or NICU stay.

Field Label: Have you had issues with your GROWTH?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Yes | 2, No | 3, Unsure
Field Note: Examples: much shorter/taller than expected for your family, lifelong problems gaining weight, or lifelong obesity.

Field Label: Have you had issues with your HORMONES and/or ENDOCRINE SYSTEM (hormone-producing glands)?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Yes | 2, No | 3, Unsure
Field Note: Examples: diabetes, estrogen/testosterone levels, or thyroid, parathyroid, or adrenal function.

Field Label: Have you ever been diagnosed with cancer?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Yes | 2, No | 3, Unsure
Field Note: Examples: breast cancer, prostate cancer, osteosarcoma, lymphoma, etc.

Field Label: Have you had issues with your HEAD/FACE/NECK?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Yes | 2, No | 3, Unsure
Field Note: Significant issues seen by a doctor or surgery. Examples: cleft lip/palate, large/small head size, fused skull bones, sparse hair. Eyes and ears are asked separately.

Field Label: Have you had issues with your EYES and/or VISION?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Yes | 2, No | 3, Unsure
Field Note: Examples: vision loss, cataracts, lazy eye, nearsighted/farsighted.

Field Label: Have you had issues with your EARS and/or HEARING?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Yes | 2, No | 3, Unsure
Field Note: Examples: hearing loss, ringing in the ears, or misshapen ears.

Field Label: Have you had issues with BRAIN and/or NERVOUS SYSTEM (neurological issues)?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Yes | 2, No | 3, Unsure
Field Note: Examples: developmental delays, intellectual disability, learning problems, seizures, muscle tone, cerebral palsy, brain structure.

Field Label: Have you had BEHAVIOR and/or PSYCHIATRIC issues?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Yes | 2, No | 3, Unsure
Field Note: Examples: autism spectrum disorders, schizophrenia, anxiety, aggression, repetitive behaviors, depression.

Field Label: Have you had issues with your SKIN?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Yes | 2, No | 3, Unsure
Field Note: Examples: unusual skin coloration, lumps/bumps needing a doctor, or sweating issues.

Field Label: Have you had issues with your BONES, CARTILAGE, and/or CONNECTIVE TISSUE?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Yes | 2, No | 3, Unsure
Field Note: Examples: easily broken bones, extreme flexibility, or look/use of arms, legs, hands, feet.

Field Label: Have you had issues with your MUSCLES?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Yes | 2, No | 3, Unsure
Field Note: Examples: muscle formation or function, including weakness, burning, or stiffness.

Field Label: Have you had issues with your HEART and/or BLOOD VESSELS?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Yes | 2, No | 3, Unsure
Field Note: Examples: cardiomyopathy, heart defects needing surgery, widened/bulging blood vessel, heart rhythm problems.

Field Label: Have you had issues with your LUNGS and/or BREATHING?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Yes | 2, No | 3, Unsure
Field Note: Examples: a collapsed lung, fluid on the lungs, or apnea.

Field Label: Have you had issues with your DIGESTIVE SYSTEM?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Yes | 2, No | 3, Unsure
Field Note: Includes stomach, intestines, or other GI tract. Examples: structure/use of stomach or intestines, extreme constipation, reflux, vomiting.

Field Label: Have you had issues with your KIDNEYS, BLADDER, and/or GENITALS (male/female reproductive parts)?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Yes | 2, No | 3, Unsure
Field Note: Examples: urination, kidneys, or reproductive system. Specific examples: hypospadias, kidney failure, infertility, incontinence.

Field Label: Have you had issues with your IMMUNE SYSTEM?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Yes | 2, No | 3, Unsure
Field Note: Examples: frequent infections or immunodeficiency.

Field Label: Have you had issues with your BLOOD and/or BLEEDING?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Yes | 2, No | 3, Unsure
Field Note: Examples: excessive bleeding or problems noted with your blood cells on lab reports.

Field Label: Have you had issues with your ORAL HEALTH?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Yes | 2, No | 3, Unsure

Section Header: Mother's Pregnancy (shown only if the pregnancy screen = Yes). Select all that apply; each selection asks time of symptoms and time of diagnosis.
Field Label: What specific MOTHER'S PREGNANCY issue(s) have you had? Select all that apply.
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Amniotic fluid issue - Too much fluid (polyhydramnios) or too little fluid (oligohydramnios) in the amniotic sac [Abnormality of the amniotic fluid; HP:0001560] | 2, Decreased fetal movement - Baby moved less than expected during pregnancy [Decreased fetal movement; HP:0001558] | 3, Delivery complication - Can include breech birth, forceps or vacuum delivery, or umbilical cord wrapped around the baby's neck [Abnormal delivery; HP:0001787] | 4, Maternal health problem - An issue with the mother's health during pregnancy; can include issues such as high blood pressure, diabetes, or CMV infection, etc. [Prenatal maternal abnormality; HP:0002686] | 5, Placental issue - Placenta was not formed, attached, or working properly [Abnormality of the placenta; HP:0100767] | 6, Pregnancy exposure - Mother had an exposure that could cause harm to the baby, like alcohol, cigarettes, street drugs, chemicals, or radiation, etc. [Maternal teratogenic exposure; HP:0011438] | 7, Prematurity - Baby was born early, before 37 weeks gestation [Premature birth; HP:0001622] | 8, Umbilical cord issue - Cord was not formed properly or had an issue like a knot [Abnormality of the umbilical cord; HP:0010881]

Section Header: Growth (shown only if the Growth screen = Yes). Select all that apply; each selection asks age of onset and age at diagnosis.

Field Label: What specific HORMONE/ENDOCRINE issue(s) have you had? Select all that apply.
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Type 2 diabetes - The most common form of diabetes, sometimes called adult, late- onset, or non-insulin dependent; may or may not require insulin [Non-insulin dependent diabetes; HP:0005978] | 2, Type 1 diabetes - Insulin-dependent diabetes, sometimes also called juvenile or autoimmune diabetes; typically requires insulin [Insulin dependent diabetes; HP:0100651] | 3, Delayed puberty - Passing the age when puberty normally occurs without any signs of physical or hormonal puberty [Delayed puberty; HP:0000823] | 4, Diabetes insipidus - Less-common form of diabetes showing extreme thirst and frequent urination [Diabetes insipidus; HP:0000873] | 5, Growth hormone deficiency [Growth hormone deficiency; HP:0000824] | 6, Early puberty - Puberty before the age of 8 years in girls or 9 years in boys; also called precocious puberty [Precocious puberty; HP:0000826] | 7, Hypogonadism - Little or no sex hormones produced by the testes in men or the ovaries in women [Hypogonadism; HP:0000135] | 8, Adrenal hyperplasia - Enlargement of the adrenal gland leading to decreased production of the hormone cortisol [Adrenal hyperplasia; HP:0008221] | 9, Hyperthyroidism - High levels of thyroid hormone, including Graves' disease [Thyroid goiter; HP:0000853] | 10, Hypothyroidism - Low levels of thyroid hormone [Hyperthyroidism; HP:0000836] | 11, Goiter - Enlargement of the thyroid gland which may result in neck swelling [Hypothyroidism; HP:0000836] | 12, Parathyroid issue - Too much or too little parathyroid hormone produced [Parathyroid dysfunction; HP:0011767]

Field Label: What specific EYES/VISION issue(s) have you had? Select all that apply.
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Eye movement issues - Eyes have unusual movement such as "lazy eye," (strabismus) or eye tremors (nystagmus), etc. [Abnormality of eye movement; HP:0000496] | 2, Difference in eye size - Eye size differs from what is usual such as a small eye size (mircophthalmia) or lack of eye development (anophthalmia), etc. [Abnormality of globe size; HP:0100887] | 3, Unusual Iris - The colored part of the eye, called the iris, has freckles/spots or eye color differs between eyes. [Abnormality of the iris; HP:0000525] | 4, Coloboma -- A notched pupil [Coloboma; HP:0000589] | 5, Lens issue - A cataract or dislocated lens (ectopia lentis), etc. [Abnormality of the lens; HP:0000517] | 6, Major vision issues - Issues may include blindness, color blindness, night vision problems, floaters, or light sensitivity, etc.; do NOT include minor nearsightedness or farsightedness. [Abnormality of vision; HP:0000504] | 7, Farsightedness - Also called hyperopia or hypermetropia; things up close look fuzzy [Myopia; HP:0000545] | 8, Nearsightedness - Also called myopia; things in the distance look fuzzy [Hypermetropia; HP:0000540] | 9, Optic nerve issues - Optic nerve is located behind the eyeball and brings information from the eye to the brain, allowing you to see; can include issues with the way this nerve was formed or the way it works [Abnormality of the optic nerve; HP:0000587] | 10, Retina issues - The retina is the layer of tissue at the back of the inner eye, and the macula is included in this category; can include issues such as retinal detachment, macular [Abnormality of the retina; HP:0000479] | 11, Ptosis - Droopiness of the upper eyelid [Ptosis; HP:0000508]

Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Ear shape difference - Part or all of the ear may be over- or under-developed; can include issues such as an ear structure that is missing or underdeveloped, a cupped ear shape, or an overly-large appearance of the ear, etc. [Abnormality of the ear; HP:0000598] | 2, Conductive hearing loss - Hearing loss due to an issue with the ear canal, ear drum, or the bones in the middle ear [Conductive hearing impairment; HP:0000405] | 3, Sensorineural hearing loss (SNHL) - Hearing loss due to an issue with the nerves in the inner ear or connecting ear to the brain [Sensorineural hearing impairment; HP:0000407] | 4, Mixed hearing loss - A combination of both conductive and sensorineural hearing loss [Mixed hearing impairment; HP:0000410] | 5, Hearing loss, unsure which type [Hearing impairment; HP:0000365] | 6, Ringing in the ears - Also called tinnitus [Tinnitus; HP:0000360] | 7, Hyperacusis - Oversensitivity to or difficulty tolerating certain sounds [Hyperacusis; HP:0010780] | 8, Dizziness - Also called vertigo [Vertigo; HP:0002321]

Field Label: What specific BRAIN/NERVOUS SYSTEM issue(s) have you had? Select all that apply.
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Brain and/or spinal cord issue - Includes problems with the formation or appearance of the brain and/or spinal cord, often found by MRI [Abnormality of the nervous system; HP:0000707] | 2, Cerebral palsy (CP) - A single damaging event to the brain that can cause movement problems, seizures, learning problems, and/or communication problems [Cerebral Palsy; HP:0100021] | 3, Cognitive impairment - Significant difficulties learning, understanding, or processing information, or making decisions [Cognitive impairment; HP:0100543] | 4, Coordination issues - Significant clumsiness or other difficulties with movement due to body parts not working together as they normally would [Abnormality of coordination; HP:0011443] | 5, EEG abnormality - A problem has been identified on an electroencephalogram, or "EEG," a test that measures the electrical activity of the brain [EEG abnormality; HP:0002353] | 6, Encephalopathy - Damage to the brain, sometimes ongoing, that results in altered brain function [Encephalopathy; HP:0001298] | 7, Hypertonia - Muscle tightness that limits movement, spasticity, increased muscle tone [Hypertonia; HP:0001276] | 8, Hypotonia - Unusually low muscle tone, muscle tone may have been described as floppy [Hypotonia; HP:0001290] | 9, Memory impairment - Increased forgetfulness or significant difficulty remembering things [Memory impairment; HP:0002354] | 10, Unusual movements - Changes or problems in the ability to move body parts voluntarily; do NOT include Parkinson's Disease here [Abnormality of movement; HP:0100022] | 11, Parkinsonism - Abnormal movements, characterized by shaking, stiffness, slowness, and difficulty walking [Parkinsonism; HP:0001300] | 12, Seizures - Also known as convulsions [Seizures; HP:0001250]

Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Atrophic scars - Scars that retract or indent [Atrophic scarring; HP:0001075] | 2, "Café-au-lait" spots - Light brown birth marks the color of coffee with milk [Multiple café-au-lait spots; HP:0007565] | 3, Hyperpigmentation - Patches of skin that are darker than the regular skin color [Hyperpigmentation; HP:0000953] | 4, Hypopigmentation - Patches of skin that are LIGHTER than the regular skin color [Hypopigmentation; HP:0001010] | 5, Decreased sweating - Also called hypohidrosis; you are not able to sweat or your ability to sweat is significantly reduced [Hypohidrosis; HP:0000966] | 6, Increased sweating - Also called "hyperhidrosis"; your sweating is significantly increased, above what is necessary to maintain a normal body temperature [Hyperhidrosis; HP:0000975] | 7, Skin fragility - Skin is fragile to touch and can include issues like ectodermal dysplasia [Fragile skin; HP:0001030] | 8, Skin sensitivity - Skin is fragile to sunlight [Skin photosensitivity; HP:0000992] | 9, Thick skin - Sometimes called keratoderma or hyperkeratosis; skin in some regions of the body is significantly thickened, often occurring on the palms of the hands or soles of the feet [Thick skin; HP:0001072] | 10, Hyperelastic skin - Skin that is extremely stretchy and returns to its original position after stretching [Hyperelastic skin; HP:0000974] | 11, Hypoelastic skin - Inelastic or "saggy" skin, often hanging in loose folds (cutis laxa) [Cutis laxa; HP:0000973]

Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Arm and/or leg bone differences since birth - Can include differences in any limb, such as a shortened arm or leg length, or the bones of the arm or leg that are formed differently, etc. [Abnormality of limb bone morphology; HP:0002813] | 2, Curvature of the spine - Spine curves side-to-side (scoliosis) or front-to-back (kyphosis) [Abnormal curvature of the spine; HP:0010674] | 3, Flexible joints - Commonly called being double-jointed, hypermobile, or hyper extensible [Joint hyperextensibility; HP:0001382] | 4, Frequent broken bones - Bones tend to break with minimal trauma, includes "brittle bone disease" [Increased susceptibility to fractures; HP:0002659] | 5, Finger and/or toe differences - Includes bony differences such as extra, missing, or fused fingers and/or toes, etc. [Abnormality of the digits; HP:0011297] | 6, Hand differences - Includes bony differences such as "split" hand or extra or missing bones in the hand. [HP:0001155] | 7, Foot differences - Includes bony differences such as "split" foot or extra or missing bones in the foot. [HP:0001760] | 8, Hip dysplasia - Issues with the formation of the hip socket, typically present at birth [Hip dysplasia; HP:0001385] | 9, Pectus carinatum - Breastbone sticks out from the chest, sometimes called "pigeon chest" [Pectus carinatum; HP:0000768] | 10, Pectus excavatum - Breastbone appears sunken or caved in to the chest, sometimes called "funnel chest" [Pectus excavatum; HP:0000767] | 11, Skeletal dysplasia - Issues or differences with the growth of the bones and/or cartilage, sometimes called dwarfism [Skeletal dysplasia; HP:0002652]

Field Label: What specific DIGESTIVE SYSTEM issue(s) have you had? Select all that apply.
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Feeding difficulties - Issues with eating; can include problems eating food by mouth or keeping food from being vomited [Feeding difficulties; HP:0011968] | 2, Esophagus - Issues with the tube that takes food from your mouth to your stomach; can include reflux, inflammation, or narrowing of the esophagus, etc. [Abnormality of the esophagus; HP:0002031] | 3, Stomach - Issues with the stomach; can include a hernia, pyloric stenosis, or problems emptying food from the stomach, etc. [Abnormality of the stomach; HP:0002577] | 4, Pancreas - Issues with pancreas such as pancreatitis or cysts [Abnormality of the pancreas; HP:0001732] | 5, Liver - Issues with liver; can include enlargement (hepatomegaly), scarring (cirrhosis), hepatitis, or cysts, etc. [Abnormality of the liver; HP:0001392] | 6, Dysmotility - Issues moving food through the digestive system; can include ileus or intestinal pseudo-obstruction, etc.; do NOT include conditions like Crohn's disease or colitis in this category [Gastrointestinal dysmotility; HP:0002579] | 7, Intestines - Issues with the small intestine, large intestine, bowel, and colon; can include malabsorption, intestinal malrotation, polyps, diverticulitis, Crohn's disease, or colitis, etc. [Abnormality of the intestine; HP:0002242] | 8, Soiling/defecation - Issues with producing a normal bowel movement (feces/poop); can include bowel incontinence, colitis, malrotation of the colon, etc. | 9, Anus - Issues with the opening between the buttocks through which solid waste (feces/poop) leaves the body such as narrowing of the anal opening or absence of the anus at birth; sometimes called anorectal malformation [Abnormality of the anus; HP:0004378]

Section Header: Kidney/Bladder/Genitals (shown only if the Kidney/Bladder/Genitals screen = Yes). Select all that apply; each selection asks age of onset and age at diagnosis.
Field Label: What specific KIDNEY/BLADDER/GENITALS issue(s) have you had? Select all that apply.
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Bladder - Issues with how the bladder is shaped or works; can include problems with bladder stones, bladder size, overactive bladder, or incontinence, etc. [Abnormality of the bladder; HP:0000014] | 2, Abnormal internal or external sex organ - Also called disorder of sex development (DSD); can include having both male and female reproductive organs, having external genitalia that is not clearly male or female, or having sex organs that do not go through normal changes at puberty [Abnormal sex determination; HP:0012244] | 3, Kidney function issue - Issue with how the kidneys work such as kidney failure or problems with proper filtering/absorption [adrenal hyperplasia; HP:0008221] | 4, Kidney structure issue - Issue with the kidney shape/structure; can include abnormal size, missing kidney, or abnormal kidney location in the body [Kidney malformation; HP:0012210] | 5, Female sex organ disorder - Issues with the formation or appearance of the female genitalia, including labia, vagina, uterus, and/or ovaries [Abnormality of FEMALE genitalia; HP:0010460] | 6, Genitalia function - Issues with the way the male or female genitalia works; can include infertility, impotence, or ovarian failure, etc. [Genital functional abnormality; HP:0000080] | 7, Male sex organ disorder - Issues with the formation or appearance of the male genitalia, including penis, scrotum, testicles, and/or prostate gland [Abnormality of MALE genitalia; HP:0010461] | 8, Urethra issue - Issues with the tube that empties the bladder; can include problems with way the urethra formed, blockage, or abnormal urethra connection (hypospadius), etc. [Abnormality of the urethra; HP:0000795] | 9, Ureter issue - Issues with tube connecting the kidney to the bladder; can include ureter size being too large or small, abnormal ureter formation or blockage, or missing ureter, etc. [Abnormality of the ureter; HP:0000069] | 10, Urine ("pee") issues - Issues with urine; can include blood or protein in urine (hematuria or proteinuria), unusual urine color, or unusual urine odor, etc. [Abnormality of the urine; HP:0003110]
